2604.05912.title: FrontierFinance: A Long-Horizon Computer-Use Benchmark of Real-World Financial Tasks
2604.05912.paper_summary: As concerns surrounding AI-driven labor displacement intensify in knowledge-intensive sectors, existing benchmarks fail to measure performance on tasks that define practical professional expertise. Finance, in particular, has been identified as a domain with high AI exposure risk, yet lacks robust benchmarks to track real-world developments. This gap is compounded by the absence of clear accountability mechanisms in current Large Language Model (LLM) deployments. To address this, we introduce FrontierFinance, a long-horizon benchmark of 25 complex financial modeling tasks across five core finance models, requiring an average of over 18 hours of skilled human labor per task to complete. Developed with financial professionals, the benchmark reflects industry-standard financial modeling workflows and is paired with detailed rubrics for structured evaluation. We engage human experts to define the tasks, create rubrics, grade LLMs, and perform the tasks themselves as human baselines. We demonstrate that our human experts both receive higher scores on average, and are more likely to provide client-ready outputs than current state-of-the-art systems.
2604.05912.paper_summary_zh: 隨著對於人工智慧驅動的勞動力取代的關注在知識密集型行業中加劇，現有的基準無法衡量定義實際專業專長的任務表現。特別是在金融領域，被認為是高人工智慧暴露風險的領域，但缺乏健全的基準來追蹤現實世界的發展。這一缺口因當前大型語言模型（LLM）部署中缺乏明確的問責機制而加劇。為了解決這個問題，我們推出了FrontierFinance，一個涵蓋五個核心金融模型的25個複雜金融建模任務的長期基準，每個任務平均需要超過18小時的熟練人力來完成。這個基準是與金融專業人士共同開發的，反映了行業標準的金融建模工作流程，並配有詳細的評分標準以便於結構化評估。我們邀請人類專家來定義任務、創建評分標準、評分LLM，並親自執行任務作為人類基準。我們展示了我們的人類專家平均得分更高，並且比當前最先進的系統更有可能提供客戶準備好的輸出。
2604.05912.author: Michael Krumdick et.al.
2604.05912.authors: Michael Krumdick, Varshini Reddy, Shivani Chaudhary, William Day, Maarij Ahmed, Hayan Haqqi, Muhammad Ahsen Fahim, Hanzallah Amjad, Ahmad Orakzai, Aqsa Gul, Chris Tanner
